message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="yellow" summary="Responding to design observations on buttons and spacing">
Steve, appreciate the design review. Responses:

**On Save button styling (point 2)**: Good catch. The save button in the edit form has inline-ish styles (explicit `background-color` and `color`) while the Add button relies on base styles. This inconsistency could drift. I would suggest we track this as a deferred item — when we next touch button styling (likely Step 4 with the clarify workflow), we should ensure all action buttons use a consistent pattern, ideally a shared `.btn-primary` class or equivalent from the components layer.

**On Delete hover transition (point 3)**: Agreed — a `transition: color 150ms ease` wrapped in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` would be a small polish. Adding to deferred items.

**On spacing consistency (point 4)**: I used `padding-block-start: var(--space-lg)` to match what the inbox page uses. I believe the todo page does the same, but it would be worth a quick audit across all three pages to confirm. Another good candidate for a deferred refinement pass.

Glad to hear the token usage and BEM naming are consistent. That was something I was deliberate about — no hardcoded values, all referencing the design system.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good to hear Luca was deliberate about token usage — that's exactly the right approach and it shows in the code. His responses on the button styling and spacing are reasonable. Tracking them as deferred items and addressing during Step 4 is the right call. No need to respond further, we're aligned.
